description: ecoTEC plus, ecoTEC pro, ecoFIT and legacy Turbomax ranges. F.xx fault codes with plain-English explanations.

faultCodes.title: Flow temperature sensor open circuit
faultCodes.summary: The PCB sees an open circuit where the flow NTC should be.
quickChecks: Re-seat the flow sensor connector
Measure NTC resistance at room temp (~10 kΩ typical)
Inspect the loom between sensor and PCB

faultCodes.title: Return temperature sensor open circuit
faultCodes.summary: Return NTC open or disconnected.
quickChecks: Re-seat the return NTC plug
Measure NTC resistance

faultCodes.title: Outdoor temperature sensor fault (open circuit)
faultCodes.summary: The outdoor temperature sensor has an open circuit. Weather compensation will not function but the boiler can still operate.
likelyCauses: Outdoor sensor cable damaged or disconnected
quickChecks: Check outdoor sensor cable and connections at the boiler
Measure sensor resistance — compare to Vaillant spec chart
If no outdoor sensor is fitted, this code can be ignored

faultCodes.title: Flow temperature sensor short to ground
faultCodes.summary: Flow NTC wiring or sensor has shorted.
quickChecks: Dry and inspect the connector
Check the wiring for damage near sharp edges
Swap in a known-good sensor

faultCodes.title: Return temperature sensor short to ground
faultCodes.summary: Return NTC wiring or sensor has shorted.
quickChecks: Inspect loom for chafing
Measure resistance — a dead short indicates sensor failure

faultCodes.title: DHW outlet sensor short circuit
faultCodes.summary: The domestic hot water outlet temperature sensor has a short circuit. Hot water temperature control may be affected.
likelyCauses: Damaged wiring between sensor and PCB
quickChecks: Measure DHW outlet sensor resistance — should be ~10kΩ at 25°C
Check connector for moisture or corrosion
Disconnect sensor and check if code clears — confirms sensor fault

faultCodes.title: Cylinder sensor short circuit
faultCodes.summary: The external hot water cylinder NTC is shorted (system boilers only).
quickChecks: Disconnect and measure sensor resistance
Check the cable run from boiler to cylinder

faultCodes.title: Return NTC sensor open circuit (cylinder sensor on system boilers)
faultCodes.summary: The cylinder NTC sensor has an open circuit. On system boilers with a cylinder connected, the boiler cannot read cylinder temperature.
likelyCauses: Cylinder sensor disconnected or cable damaged
quickChecks: Check wiring between cylinder sensor pocket and boiler
Measure sensor resistance — should be ~10kΩ at 25°C
If no cylinder fitted (combi), check diagnostic setting D.017

faultCodes.title: Outdoor temperature sensor short circuit
faultCodes.summary: The outdoor temperature sensor has a short circuit. Weather compensation will revert to a fixed flow temperature.
likelyCauses: Short circuit in sensor cable (water ingress)
quickChecks: Inspect outdoor sensor cable for damage or water ingress
Disconnect sensor at the boiler and see if code clears
Measure resistance — a short will read near 0Ω

faultCodes.title: Safety shut-off, overheat
faultCodes.summary: Overheat thermostat or flow sensor has exceeded the safe limit.
likelyCauses: Limescale or sludge in the heat exchanger
quickChecks: Verify pump operation
Vent the system and auto air vent
Measure overheat stat continuity cold

faultCodes.title: Low water pressure / dry fire protection
faultCodes.summary: Pressure sensor reads below the operating threshold or the PCB has detected a dry run condition.
likelyCauses: Filling loop not fully closed or dripping
quickChecks: Top up the system to 1.2 bar cold
Inspect pressure sensor connection
Check for visible leaks
If no hot water but heating works, check the diverter valve — commonly sticks on Turbomax models

faultCodes.title: Flow and return temperature differential too large
faultCodes.summary: Big gap between flow and return suggests poor circulation.
quickChecks: Bleed rads and auto air vent
Confirm isolation valves fully open
Feel flow and return pipes for temperature split

faultCodes.title: Temperature rise too fast
faultCodes.summary: Rate of change on flow temp is too high — starved circulation at the sensor.
quickChecks: Check pump runs at call for heat
Bleed the auto air vent
Open any closed flow-side valves

faultCodes.title: Flue gas temperature too high
faultCodes.summary: Flue stat or flue NTC reading above safe limit.
quickChecks: Inspect flue terminal and condensate drain points
Check flue NTC in service mode
Service heat exchanger if overdue

faultCodes.title: Flame detected when gas valve closed
faultCodes.summary: Parasitic flame signal — electrode tracking or gas valve passing.
quickChecks: Dry and inspect lead and electrode
Soap-test the burner with gas valve closed

faultCodes.title: Ignition failure on start-up
faultCodes.summary: Boiler tried to light but the flame was never established.
likelyCauses: Blocked condensate trap drowning the burner
quickChecks: Confirm gas on at other appliances
Inspect electrode and lead
Measure inlet working pressure
Drain the condensate trap
On Turbomax models, if F.28 only occurs on DHW demand, check the diverter valve position and operation

faultCodes.title: Flame lost during operation
faultCodes.summary: Flame lit but was extinguished mid-cycle.
quickChecks: Check working gas pressure under full load
Inspect flue for draughts or wind cut-out
Clean flame-sense electrode

faultCodes.title: Fan fault
faultCodes.summary: Fan not matching commanded speed.
quickChecks: Listen for rough running
Inspect the impeller for debris
Check 230V supply and tacho signal

faultCodes.title: Air pressure switch fault (older models)
faultCodes.summary: Air pressure switch has not confirmed flow on older pre-ecoTEC models.
quickChecks: Inspect the APS hose for moisture
Swap the APS if proven faulty
Check flue for blockage

faultCodes.title: Insufficient fresh air supply fault
faultCodes.summary: The boiler has detected insufficient combustion air. This is a safety shutdown to prevent incomplete combustion.
likelyCauses: Flue terminal blocked (birds' nest, debris, leaves)
Flue run too long or too many bends
Air intake vent blocked (room-sealed boiler needs clear intake)
quickChecks: Check the flue terminal outside — is it clear and unobstructed?
Inspect the full flue run for damage, separation, or blockage
Ensure no building work has blocked the flue exit path

faultCodes.title: Flue blocked / exhaust fault
faultCodes.summary: The flue gas discharge is being recirculated or blocked, causing the boiler to lock out. Common after building work near the flue terminal.
likelyCauses: Flue terminal blocked or too close to a wall/recess
Flue has become separated inside the wall
Wind guard missing on exposed sites
quickChecks: Inspect the flue terminal — is it clear of any obstructions?
Check the flue hasn't separated at any joints
If exposed location, check if a wind guard is required

faultCodes.title: Coding resistor / product code fault
faultCodes.summary: The boiler's internal coding resistor is reading incorrectly. The boiler cannot determine its configuration.
likelyCauses: Coding resistor connector loose or corroded
Incorrect coding resistor fitted after PCB replacement
quickChecks: Check the coding resistor connector on the PCB
Verify the correct resistor value for the boiler model
If PCB was recently replaced, ensure the correct coding resistor was transferred

faultCodes.title: eBUS voltage fault
faultCodes.summary: Low or short-circuited voltage on the eBUS controller cable.
likelyCauses: Damaged cable to the room controller
quickChecks: Disconnect the eBUS cable at the boiler — if fault clears, issue is downstream
Inspect cable run for crushed/stapled areas
Swap in a known-good controller

faultCodes.title: Flame supervision fault
faultCodes.summary: The flame monitoring electrode is not detecting a satisfactory flame signal. The boiler locks out intermittently.
likelyCauses: Flame sense electrode dirty or glazed
Gas pressure too low — weak flame can't reach electrode
quickChecks: Clean the flame sense electrode with fine emery cloth
Check gas pressure at burner — minimum 17.5 mbar dynamic for natural gas
Verify electrode position — gap should be per manufacturer spec

faultCodes.title: Gas pressure fault
faultCodes.summary: Low gas pressure detected by the pressure switch.
quickChecks: Verify inlet pressure at the boiler test point under full rate
Check for other gas appliances dropping pressure

faultCodes.title: Gas valve control fault
faultCodes.summary: PCB cannot control the gas valve correctly.
likelyCauses: Gas valve coil open or shorted
quickChecks: Measure gas valve coil resistance
Check continuity between valve connector and PCB

faultCodes.title: Gas valve shut-off delay
faultCodes.summary: Gas valve did not close quickly enough at end of cycle.
quickChecks: Soap-test valve closure
Clean flame-sense electrode

faultCodes.title: EEPROM fault
faultCodes.summary: PCB memory error.
quickChecks: Power-cycle the boiler
Replace PCB if persistent

faultCodes.title: A/D converter fault
faultCodes.summary: Internal sensor-reading hardware fault on the PCB.
quickChecks: Unplug flow and return NTCs to isolate — if fault clears, suspect the sensor
Otherwise replace PCB

faultCodes.title: PCB temperature too high
faultCodes.summary: PCB temperature sensor above limit.
likelyCauses: Boiler in overheated casing or cupboard
quickChecks: Let the boiler cool
Check for poor ventilation in the install location

faultCodes.title: Flame signal implausible
faultCodes.summary: Flame signal does not match expected range.
quickChecks: Clean the flame-sense electrode
Verify earth continuity back to the PCB

faultCodes.title: Flame signal unstable
faultCodes.summary: Flame signal fluctuates more than allowed during operation.
quickChecks: Check gas working pressure under load
Replace worn electrode
Inspect flue terminal

faultCodes.title: Invalid product code (DSN)
faultCodes.summary: Display/PCB combination does not have a valid product code programmed.
likelyCauses: New PCB or display fitted without programming
quickChecks: Programme the DSN using the diagnostic menu as per the service manual

faultCodes.title: Flow temperature sensor signal fault
faultCodes.summary: Flow NTC reading implausible — stuck at a fixed value.
likelyCauses: Flow NTC failed shorted or drifted
Sensor not in thermal contact with pipe
quickChecks: Swap in a known-good flow NTC
Verify sensor is clipped tight to the pipe

faultCodes.title: Flow/return sensor plausibility
faultCodes.summary: Flow and return sensors are not giving a coherent pair of readings.
quickChecks: Compare both sensors cold — should read within a degree or two
Confirm sensors are on the correct pipes

faultCodes.title: Water pressure sensor open circuit or short
faultCodes.summary: Pressure transducer signal out of range.
quickChecks: Re-seat connector
Measure sensor supply and signal
Swap sensor if required

faultCodes.title: Pressure sensor implausible
faultCodes.summary: Pressure sensor reading does not vary as expected.
likelyCauses: Blocked port on the pressure sensor
quickChecks: Compare reading on display with system gauge
Replace sensor if stuck at a fixed value

faultCodes.title: Pump or pressure sensor fault (no pressure change on pump start)
faultCodes.summary: Pump starts but sensor never sees the expected pressure rise. This is one of the most common Vaillant fault codes — often caused by a stuck pump or a blocked pressure sensor especially on the ecoTEC plus.
quickChecks: Vent the system thoroughly
Confirm pump is actually turning
Inspect pressure sensor port for debris
Tap the pump gently — if it frees and boiler fires, the pump was stuck. Clean or replace.

faultCodes.title: Primary heat exchanger fuse tripped
faultCodes.summary: Overheat cut-out on the heat exchanger has opened.
quickChecks: Investigate cause of overheat (poor circulation, limescale) before replacing the fuse element or heat exchanger

faultCodes.title: Flue non-return valve fault
faultCodes.summary: On shared flue systems (twin-pipe), the non-return valve feedback is incorrect.
quickChecks: Inspect the flue non-return flap
Check microswitch operation

faultCodes.title: Flow/return temperature change too small
faultCodes.summary: Boiler has fired but temperature is not rising as expected.
likelyCauses: Sensors not in contact with the pipes
quickChecks: Confirm sensors are clipped and thermal paste intact
Verify system is full and vented

faultCodes.title: Flow/return temperature plausibility
faultCodes.summary: Flow and return are showing an illogical relationship (e.g. return hotter than flow).
quickChecks: Confirm which sensor is on which pipe
Swap sensor positions if necessary

faultCodes.title: Flow/return sensors wrongly fitted
faultCodes.summary: Related to F.84 — both sensors may be on the same pipe.
likelyCauses: Sensors both clipped to flow or both to return
quickChecks: Re-fit sensors to the correct pipes per the service manual
